notes: Pickleheads lists exactly 4 locations / 17 courts for San Ramon (slug: san-ramon), which is the sum of this file's 4 venues (4 Central Park + 3 Sports Basement + 6 Iron Horse Gym + 4 ClubSport = 17) using Pickleheads' own per-venue figures. The City of San Ramon's own official recreation pages (sanramon.ca.gov -- the primary source for this research) only describe 2 of these 4 venues in real detail: San Ramon Central Park (city-owned, free, dedicated outdoor courts) and Iron Horse Gym (a city-run seasonal indoor open-gym program). That's expected, not a gap -- the other two, Sports Basement San Ramon and ClubSport San Ramon, are privately operated (a retail store's outdoor courtyard and a membership athletic club) and simply aren't part of city parks & rec's own inventory. Cross-checked against Pickleheads, the City's own site, DailyPB, Pickleballify, PicklePlay (which announced it is shutting down Nov 26, 2025), Bounce, Global Pickleball Network, Yelp, and each operator's own site. No 5th real San Ramon venue turned up anywhere -- Yelp's 'pickleball near San Ramon' search and Global Pickleball Network's city court-list both pad out with clearly out-of-city results (Walnut Creek, Hayward, San Jose, Brentwood, etc.) once true local results run out, which confirms San Ramon has no hidden additional venue rather than this research having missed one. The one genuine open question is Iron Horse Gym's exact court count (6 vs. 3 vs. 9 depending on source) -- see that venue's notes; every other venue's core facts are consistently corroborated across independent sources.

venues.name: San Ramon Central Park
venues.surface: Outdoor concrete, dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets
venues.hours: Dawn-10pm daily; lights for evening play dusk-10pm, activated via a button on the light post at the center of the courts
venues.weather: Inland San Ramon Valley (Tri-Valley) — hot, dry, sunny summers with afternoon highs regularly in the 90s°F (occasional 100°F+ heat waves), sheltered from coastal fog by the hills; mild, wetter winters. Best outdoor play is early morning or evening June-September.
venues.notes: The city's own primary source (its Pickleball page + its Central Park facility page) and Pickleheads agree exactly on every core fact: 4 outdoor dedicated concrete courts, free, first-come-first-served walk-up only (cannot be reserved), dawn-10pm with dusk-10pm lights. DailyPB corroborates. Central Park is a large 40.8-acre park with separate tennis courts (3, per a third-party tennis/pickleball directory), a basketball court, and a volleyball court distinct from the 4 dedicated pickleball courts -- not the same striped surface, so no double-counting risk. Address note for anyone re-checking: the city's own Pickleball page's site-wide contact footer surfaces San Ramon City Hall's address (7000 Bollinger Canyon Rd) rather than the park's; Central Park's real address (12501 Alcosta Blvd) is independently confirmed via the city's own Central Park page, Yelp, Waze, and Apple Maps, and is what's used here. Rules stated on the official page: non-marking rubber-soled shoes required; glass containers, pets, food, bicycles, skateboards, and amplified sound are prohibited; city-sponsored programs get priority access; private lessons/tournaments need city authorization; a paddle-holder system manages the waiting rotation (not the same as a stated wait time, so waitTime is still left 'Not specified'). lat/lon and the googleMapsUrl cid were derived from a live Google Maps directions URL keyed to this exact address.

venues.name: Sports Basement San Ramon
venues.surface: Outdoor concrete ("The Courtyard"), permanent court lines
venues.hours: Store hours Mon-Fri 11am-8pm, Sat-Sun 9am-6pm. 2 of the 3 courts are free to reserve Mon-Wed 11am-7:30pm & Thu 11am-5pm; the 3rd court (and all 3 outside reserved windows) is first-come drop-in.
amenities: Retail pro shop (paddles, balls, gear rentals)
venues.weather: Inland San Ramon Valley (Tri-Valley) — hot, dry, sunny summers with afternoon highs regularly in the 90s°F (occasional 100°F+ heat waves), sheltered from coastal fog by the hills; mild, wetter winters. Best outdoor play is early morning or evening June-September.
venues.notes: Sports Basement is a retail outdoor-gear chain; this location converted a former Orchard Supply Hardware lot in 2016 and built 'The Courtyard,' an outdoor pickleball area that's open to the public for free, not just customers. Pickleheads and Sports Basement's own site (a genuinely independent, first-party source) agree exactly: 3 outdoor concrete courts, permanent lines with portable nets, free, 2 courts reservable in specific windows plus a 3rd always-drop-in court. Not on the City of San Ramon's own recreation pages at all -- it's private property, not a city facility, so that's expected rather than a gap. No specific booking-page URL was found for the reservation system beyond Sports Basement's general site, so bookingUrl is left null rather than guessed at. Coordinates and the googleMapsUrl cid come from a live, uniquely-titled 'Sports Basement San Ramon' Google Maps place link.

venues.name: Iron Horse Gym
venues.surface: Indoor wood gym floor (multi-use gymnasium; floors A/B/C combine to full size); pickleball lines added for open-gym sessions
venues.price: $7 per person per session, credit card only, no cash accepted (per City of San Ramon and DailyPB); Pickleheads states $6 per player instead -- unresolved conflict, see notes
venues.hours: Seasonal Open Gym Pickleball, Sundays only; exact time varies by season/listing (seen as both 4:30-7pm and 6:30-9:30pm across different listings) -- confirm the current session time via the City of San Ramon's Open Gym page or teamsideline.com/sites/sanramon before visiting
venues.weather: Inland San Ramon Valley (Tri-Valley) — hot, dry, sunny summers with afternoon highs regularly in the 90s°F (occasional 100°F+ heat waves), sheltered from coastal fog by the hills; mild, wetter winters. Indoor court, so less weather-dependent than the city's outdoor venues.
venues.notes: Real, currently-operating venue -- existence and 'Indoor' status are solid (the city's own Open Gym and Iron Horse Community Gym pages both confirm seasonal pickleball runs in this gymnasium; Meetup lists recurring 'Pickleball Open Gym' events here on Sundays). But the exact court count is a genuine 3-way conflict: Pickleheads (and DailyPB/Bounce, which likely echo it) say 6; PicklePlay says 3 (PicklePlay itself announced it was shutting down Nov 26, 2025, so its figure may be stale); Pickleballify says 9. Recorded 6 here because it's Pickleheads' own stated per-venue figure, and it's the only one of the three that makes Pickleheads' city-level total add up exactly (4 Central Park + 3 Sports Basement + 6 Iron Horse + 4 ClubSport = 17, matching Pickleheads' own stated city total) -- but that's inference, not a source directly resolving the conflict, so confirmed is left false pending human verification (e.g. a call to Parks & Community Services at the contact given on the city's Open Gym page, Jessica Pineda-Prieto, JPineda-Prieto@sanramon.ca.gov). Same treatment for price: the City's own materials and DailyPB say $7/person, credit-card-only, no cash; Pickleheads alone says $6 -- also left as an unresolved conflict rather than picked. The city's own Open Gym page describes this as 'seasonal pickleball,' one of several open-gym sports (badminton, volleyball, basketball) rotating through the gym, which likely explains why hours vary across sources/time periods rather than being a fixed year-round schedule. PicklePlay separately describes this venue as requiring 'membership' -- that contradicts every other source (city, Pickleheads, DailyPB, Meetup, Nextdoor all describe simple drop-in entry) and PicklePlay is shutting down, so that claim looks like stale/bad data and was not carried into the price field. Venue is officially 'Iron Horse Community Gym' / 'Iron Horse Community Gymnasium,' on the Iron Horse Middle School campus (San Ramon Valley Unified School District); 'Iron Horse Gym' (used here) is Pickleheads' and most aggregators' shorthand name. lat/lon left null with approxLocation:true -- the street address (12601 Alcosta Blvd) is solid, but no source gave a precise geocode distinct from the address itself, and WebFetch on Google Maps/pickleheads.com is blocked, so no coordinate digits were invented. googleMapsUrl left null for the same reason -- no numeric cid was found for this address, only an unrelated business's ChIJ-format place_id, which per this file's sourcing rule cannot substitute for a real cid.

venues.name: ClubSport San Ramon
venues.address: 350 Bollinger Canyon Ln, San Ramon, CA 94582
venues.surface: Outdoor concrete, dedicated courts with permanent lines
venues.price: Membership required (specific fee not stated); drop-in classes and 1:1 coaching also offered
venues.hours: General club hours: Mon-Thu 5am-10pm, Fri 5am-9pm, Sat 6am-9pm, Sun 7am-9pm (facility-wide hours, not confirmed as pickleball-court-specific; members reserve courts up to 3 days ahead via member login)
venues.weather: Inland San Ramon Valley (Tri-Valley) — hot, dry, sunny summers with afternoon highs regularly in the 90s°F (occasional 100°F+ heat waves), sheltered from coastal fog by the hills; mild, wetter winters. Best outdoor play is early morning or evening June-September.
venues.notes: Private membership athletic club (part of the multi-location ClubSport chain -- this repo already has separate clubsport-fremont and clubsport-of-pleasanton entries for sibling locations in other cities). Not on the City of San Ramon's own recreation pages -- expected, since it's a private business, not a city facility. Pickleheads spells this venue 'Club Sport San Ramon' (two words); every other source checked (the operator's own clubsportsr.com, Yelp, Facebook, Nextdoor) spells it 'ClubSport' as one word, which is used here as the canonical name -- worth knowing if cross-referencing the pickleheadsUrl, which keeps Pickleheads' two-word slug. Court count (4 outdoor concrete courts, portable nets on permanent lines) is stated consistently by Pickleheads and Bounce with no contradicting figure found anywhere, and the club's own site independently confirms it operates dedicated pickleball courts with member reservations (up to 3 days ahead) plus drop-in beginner/intermediate classes and 1:1 coaching -- but the '4' figure itself was only directly read from Pickleheads/Bounce, not independently re-stated in so many words on clubsportsr.com's own page (WebFetch on clubsportsr.com returned 403 both times it was tried, consistent with this domain generally blocking automated fetches), so treat the count with slightly less certainty than Central Park's or Sports Basement's, even though it's marked confirmed here since no source disagrees. Hours shown are the club's general operating hours from Pickleheads/clubsportsr.com, not confirmed as pickleball-court-specific. googleMapsUrl left null -- only a ChIJ-format Google place_id (ChIJc-kfIH3yj4ARiJhShU_IIp4) was found, not a numeric cid, and per this file's sourcing rule a place_id cannot substitute for one. lat/lon (37.772067, -121.944666) were returned identically by two independently-phrased searches, one of which did not hint at the coordinate value, giving reasonable confidence -- but neither surfaced the raw Google Maps source URL directly (only aggregator sites that already had it in their own structured data), so approxLocation is set true rather than treating it as a directly-confirmed pin like the two city-run venues above.
